shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,L  -> value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE; cmplog AND naive BLOCK. vp-PRO gradient family: the CMP_MAP distance gradient resolves where I2S substitution and edge-only feedback cannot.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (vp,vpc resolve / cmp,naive block). This evidence test is the INDEPENDENT verification (G2): it measures whether vp-resolving seeds CLOSE THE DISTANCE to the gradient target while cmplog-blocking seeds STALL, which is logically independent of the resolve pattern that named the shape.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient drives its seeds measurably closer to the gate's target value than cmplog/naive seeds get, while I2S substitution does not close the distance

measurement.tool_status: NOT YET BUILT - vp-gradient-climb descriptor. Orchestrator must build it. Proposed: for the branch's gradient cmp-site, take the per-seed MINIMUM operand-distance (Hamming for fixed-width literal/integer compares; matching-prefix-length complement for string/keyword compares; character-class boundary distance for range gates) between a seed's candidate operand and the gate target, over the resolving-seed set (W = value_profile + value_profile_cmplog resolving seeds) vs the blocking-seed set (L = cmplog + naive blocking seeds). The target value comes from the signature's operand_literal where present, else from the per-role overlay's gated CMP. Distance normalized to [0,1] (0 = exact match reached).

metric_defs.vp_min_dist: median over W-resolving seeds of the per-seed minimum normalized operand-distance to the gate target (how close vp's corpus got)
metric_defs.cmp_min_dist: median over L-blocking seeds of the per-seed minimum normalized operand-distance to the gate target (how close cmplog/naive's corpus got)
metric_defs.dist_gap: cmp_min_dist - vp_min_dist (positive => vp closed the distance the losers could not)

params.dispersed_targets: remaining branches use the per-role-overlay gated CMP operand (keyword/token literal: xmlns, vacuum, SELECT-with-shift, dotted-name); distance = complement of longest matching prefix of the gated token, since byte-offset is not fixed across differently-sized seeds (data_realities: structural-assembly gates have different-sized W/L seeds, byte-alignment fails).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if the vp-PRO CMP_MAP-gradient story is true, vp's resolving corpus reaches operands strictly closer to the gate target than the losers (vp_min_dist << cmp_min_dist, dist_gap clearly positive). The ALTERNATIVES this rules out: (a) plain coverage luck would predict dist_gap ~ 0 (vp got no closer, it just resolved by chance / corpus volume); (b) an I2S-substitution story would predict cmplog ALSO closes the distance (cmp_min_dist ~ 0) since the target operand would be lifted from a logged CMP - so cmplog NOT closing the distance discriminates gradient from I2S.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's gradient-built corpus reaches a deeper / later parse state than cmplog/naive, corroborating the climb when value-distance is unavailable or the gate is a multi-stage parse depth rather than a single operand

measurement.tool_status: NOT YET BUILT - depth_reach descriptor. Orchestrator must build it. Proposed: over the campaign coverage_timeseries for the branch's involved fuzzers, measure how deep into the gating call-chain each fuzzer's corpus penetrates by the final checkpoint - the count (or fraction) of on-path predecessor branches between the parse entry and the blocker that the fuzzer covers. vp_depth = median for value_profile+vpc; cmp_depth = median for cmplog+naive.

metric_defs.vp_depth: median fraction of on-path predecessor gate-blocks covered by the value_profile/vpc corpus
metric_defs.cmp_depth: median fraction of on-path predecessor gate-blocks covered by the cmplog/naive corpus
metric_defs.depth_lift: vp_depth - cmp_depth (positive => vp's corpus climbs deeper along the gating chain)

params.applies_to: multi-stage parse-path / derived-structural-state branches where the gate is a parse DEPTH, not a single fixed operand (libxml2 prologue/DTD/namespace, sqlite3 chained-parser/two-part-name/codegen).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: a true gradient climb means vp's corpus penetrates further down the on-path predecessor chain than the losers (depth_lift > 0). The alternative ruled out: if vp resolved by a single lucky jump rather than a climb, vp and cmplog corpora would reach equal intermediate depth (depth_lift ~ 0) and only the final blocker would differ - this is the 'no real gradient' null.

data_realities_respected: On-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 -> the value_distance (seed_set) test can only SCORE the 6 sqlite3 branches (sqlite3_14437/14475/14479/14589/14623/14675). The 11 lcms+libxml2 branches have no on-disk corpus; they rely on the coverage_timeseries depth_reach test (campaign coverage exists for all targets) and otherwise carry to fallback=inconclusive.

I2S/VP untagged in lineage: neither test reads a 'gradient event'. value_distance reads only the saved CORPUS operand values; depth_reach reads only coverage. VP's effect is inferred from what the corpus REACHED (distance / depth), never from a mutation-op or lineage event.

Structural-assembly / dispersed gates have different-sized W vs L seeds -> NO byte-offset alignment for those; distance uses matching-prefix-complement of the gated token, not fixed-offset Hamming. Only the 3 fixed-offset branches (lcms_2143 fourcc, libxml2_6466 single-byte ';', libxml2_7143 char-class) use offset-anchored Hamming.
Both tests are INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): they measure operand-distance / parse-depth in the corpus, not whether vp/vpc resolved the blocker.
notes: MERGES: collapsed the 5 prior Pass-B subtypes to ONE discriminable hypothesis + ONE corroborating fallback. The 5 prior labels (literal_token_assembly, multistage_parse_path, single_byte_boundary, namespace_structure_path, derived_structural_state) are SOURCE-SEMANTIC distinctions (what kind of token/depth the gradient builds: a FOURCC vs an xmlns cascade vs a dotted name vs a parse depth) - NO real-data measurement can separate them, because every one of them produces the SAME observable: vp's corpus closes the operand-distance to a gate target that cmplog/naive cannot. They differ only in WHICH target and HOW it is encoded, which is a params detail (fixed_offset_targets vs dispersed_targets), not a separate mechanism. So they collapse into vp_gradient_value_distance_closed. The single_byte_boundary subtype is the cleanest INSTANCE of the same mechanism (fixed-offset, exactly byte-alignable) - kept as a params subclass, not a separate hypothesis. TARGETS: of 18 branches, only 6 (sqlite3) have on-disk corpus for the value_distance test; lcms_2143 + the 10 libxml2 branches must rely on depth_reach (coverage exists for all targets) or fall to inconclusive - this is a genuine data-coverage limitation, not a design choice. TOOL BUILD REQUIRED: both descriptors are NOT YET BUILT (registry_tool=null). vp_gradient_value_distance_closed needs a vp-gradient-climb seed_set:value_distance_reached tool (the registry's joint_necessity measures struct-size/tokens, NOT operand-distance - the value-precision joint subtype is explicitly noted as joint_necessity's blind spot, and that gap is exactly this family's signal). vp_gradient_corpus_reaches_deeper needs a coverage_timeseries:depth_reach tool. The orchestrator owns building/registering both; both descriptors stay in the closed vocab so they dedup at build with any sibling-shape value_distance / depth_reach descriptors. DISCRIMINATION CAVEAT: the value_distance test's power rests on cmplog NOT closing the distance - if a branch's gate target operand happens to be liftable by I2S (appears verbatim in a logged CMP), cmplog could close it too and dist_gap collapses; for those the test correctly returns inconclusive rather than mis-confirming gradient. The shape's Layer-1 evidence (cmplog is a LOSER) predicts I2S does NOT suffice here, so dist_gap is expected positive - that consistency is itself the cross-check.
